content_of_post_to_answer: Does a character's personality actually change once they pass the thousand-year mark?
answer: A thousand years would not automatically make a character wiser or less emotional; it would mostly change what their emotions are attached to. An immortal who has watched generations disappear may become patient, detached, or strangely sentimental, but their core traits can remain intact. The interesting shift is not personality being replaced, but priorities being reshaped by extreme experience. Time does not erase identity; it reveals which parts of identity were temporary.
